questions.q: Let $A B$ : $y=x$ be a chord to the parabola $y^2+4 x=4$ and $P$ be any variable point on the ellipse $x^2+9(y-4)^2-9=0$. If $\Delta_1$ and $\Delta_2$ be the minimum and maximum area of triangle ABP respectively then
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If two distinct chords of a parabola $x^2=4$ ay passing through $(2 a, a)$ are bisected on the line $x+y=1$, then length of latus rectum can be -
questions.solution:
Any point on the line $x+y=1$ can be taken as $(\lambda, 1-\lambda)$ so equation of the chord taking this point as mid point will be
$\lambda x-2(y+1-\lambda)=\lambda^2-4 a(1-\lambda)$
It passes through $(2 a, a)$
$\begin{aligned}
& 2 \mathrm{a} \lambda-2 \mathrm{a}(\mathrm{a}+1-\lambda)=\lambda^2-4 \mathrm{a}(1-\lambda) \\
& \Rightarrow \lambda^2-2 \mathrm{a}+2 \mathrm{a}^2=0
\end{aligned}$
It has two distinct root then
$\begin{aligned} & -\left(-2 \mathrm{a}+2 \mathrm{a}^2\right)\gt0 \\ & \mathrm{a}^2-\mathrm{a} \lt 0 \Rightarrow 0 \lt \mathrm{a} \lt 1\end{aligned}$


$\text { length of latus rectum can be between } 0 \text { and } 4 \text {. }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If PQ is a chord of parabola $x^2=4 y$ which subtends right angle at vertex. Then locus of centroid of triangle PSQ ( S is focus) is a parabola whose -
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } \mathrm{P}\left(2 \mathrm{t}_1 \cdot \mathrm{t}_1^2\right), \mathrm{Q}\left(2 \mathrm{t}_2 \cdot \mathrm{t}_2^2\right) \\ & \mathrm{m}_{\mathrm{OP}} \mathrm{m}_{\mathrm{OQ}}=-1 \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{t}_1}{2} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{t}_2}{2}=-1 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2=-4\end{aligned}$ Let centroid $G(h, k)$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { so } \mathrm{h}=\frac{2 \mathrm{t}_1+2 \mathrm{t}_2}{3}, \mathrm{k}=\frac{\mathrm{t}_1^2+\mathrm{t}_2^2+1}{3} \\ & 3 \mathrm{k}-1=\left(\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2\right)^2-2 \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2 \\ & \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{k}-1=\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{~h}}{2}\right)^2+8 \\ & \Rightarrow 3(\mathrm{k}-3) \cdot 4=9 \mathrm{~h}^2 \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{x}^2=4(\mathrm{y}-3) \end{aligned}$ so vertex $(0,3), \quad \mathrm{LR}=\frac{4}{3}$, axis $\mathrm{x}=0$, tangent at vertex $\mathrm{y}=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $P Q$ is a double ordinate of the parabola $y^2=4 a x$. If the normal at $P$ intersect the line passing through $Q$ and parallel to $x$-axis at $G$ then locus of $G$ is a parabola with
options: length of latus rectum equal to 4a
questions.solution: $N: y+t x=2 a t+a t^3$ $L: y=-2 a t$ On solving together $-2 a t+t x=2 a t+a t^3$ $x=a t^2+4 a$ Hence $h=a t^2+4 a....(i)$ $k=-2 a t....(ii)$ So $y^2=4 a(x-4 a)$ (eliminating $\left.t\right)$ $\qquad$ Now verify alternatives
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A circle 'S' is described on the focal chord of the parabola $y^2=4 x$ as diameter. If the focal chord is inclined at an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with axis of x, then which of the following is/are true
options: the circle $x^2+y^2+2 x-6 y+3=0$ is orthogonal to ' $S$ '
questions.solution: Circle on PQ as diameter $\begin{aligned} & \left(x-t_1^2\right)\left(x-t_2^2\right)+\left(y-2 t_1\right)\left(y-2 t_2\right)=0 \\ & x^2+y^2-\left(t_1^2+t_2^2\right) x-2\left(t_1+t_2\right) y+t_1^2 t_2^2+4 t_1 t_2=0\end{aligned}$ Put $\frac{t_1+t_2}{2}=1$ and $t_1 t_2=-1$ Hence equation of circle become $x^2+y^2-6 x-4 y-3=0$ ...(1) With centre (3,2) and radius 4 Equation (1) is not orthogonal to $x^2+y^2+2 x-6 y+3=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let two parabolas be $S_1: y^2=4 a x$ and $S_2: y^2=-4 a x$. From any point $P$ on $S_1$, tangents are drawn to $S_2$ touching it at $Q$ and $R$, then:
options: line $Q R$ neither touches nor intersect $S_1$
if normal at any point $A(t)$ on $S_1$ is tangent to $S_2$ then $t^2=\sqrt{2}-1$
if normal at any point $A(t)$ on $S_1$ is tangent to $S_2$ then $t^2=\sqrt{2}+1$
questions.solution:
Equation of chord of contact $Q R$ from $P\left(a t^2, 2 a t\right)$ will be $y \cdot 2 a t=-2 a\left(x+a t^2\right)$
$\Rightarrow y(-2 a t)=2 a\left(x+a t^2\right)$ which is tangent to parabola at $y^2=4 a x$ at $\left(a t^2,-2 a t\right)$ and normal at $A(t)$ on $S_1 \Rightarrow y+t x=2 a t+a t^3$
$\Rightarrow \quad y=-t x+2 a t+a t^3$   ...(1)
Tangent at $S_2$ will be : $y=m x-\frac{a}{m}$  ...(2)
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad m=-t \text { and } 2 a t+a t^3=-\frac{a}{m} \\ & \therefore \quad 2 a t+a t^3=\frac{a}{t} \quad \Rightarrow \quad t^4+2 t^2=1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad\left(t^2+1\right)^2=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A parabola $C$ whose focus is $S(0,0)$ and passing through $P(3,4)$. Equation of tangent at $P$ to parabola is $3 x+4 y-25=0$. A chord through $S$ parallel to tangent at $P$ intersects the parabola at $A$ and $B$. Which of the following are CORRECT ?
options: Latus rectum of parabola is 20 units
Only one real normal can be drawn from the point ( $-3,-4$ )
Only one real normal can be drawn from the point $(-6,-8)$
questions.solution:
The tangent $3 x+4 y-25=0$ is tangent at vertex and axis is $4 x$ $-3 y=0$
So, $\mathrm{PS}=\mathrm{a}=5$
Latus rectum $=\mathrm{AB}=20$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The parabola $x=y^2+a y+b$ intersect the parabola $x^2=y \quad$ at $(1,1)$ at right angle. Which of the following is/are correct?
options: Equation of the director circle for the parabola $x=y^2+a y+b$ is $4 x+1=0$.
Area enclosed by the parabola $x=y^2+a y+b$ and its latus rectum is $\frac{1}{6}$
questions.solution:
We have $x=y^2+a y+b$ $\qquad ....(1)$ $\text { and } x^2=y$ $\qquad ....(2)$ As (1) passing through $(1,1)$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 1=1+a+b \\ & \Rightarrow a+b=0 \end{aligned}$ Now, $1=2 \mathrm{y} \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\mathrm{a} \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}$ $\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right]_{\left(x_1, y_1\right)}=\frac{1}{2+a}$ and for $\left.y=x^2, \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=2 x \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\right]_{(1,1)}=2$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \frac{2}{2+a}=-1 \\ & \Rightarrow-2=2+a \\ & \Rightarrow a=-4 \text { and } b=4 \end{aligned}$ Hence parabola is $\mathrm{y}^2-4 \mathrm{y}+4=\mathrm{x} \Rightarrow(\mathrm{y}-2)^2=\mathrm{x}$ Let $\mathrm{y}-2=\mathrm{Y}$
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{Y}^2=\mathrm{X}$ Hence Required area $=2 \int_0^{\frac{1}{4}} \sqrt{x} d x=(2)\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)\left(x^{\frac{3}{2}}\right)_0^{\frac{1}{4}}=\frac{1}{6}$ sq. units Ans. $\Rightarrow$ (D) Equation of directrix is $x=\frac{-1}{4}$ $\Rightarrow 4 x+1=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The locus of the mid point of the focal radii of a variable point moving on the parabola, $y^2=4 a x$ is a parabola whose
options: Latus rectum is half the latus rectum of the original parabola
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & h=\frac{a+\alpha}{2}, k=\frac{\beta}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \alpha=2 h-a, \beta=2 k \end{aligned}$ $\alpha, \beta$ satisfies the parabola $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \beta^2=4 a \alpha \\ & 4 k^2=4 a(2 n-a) \\ & y^2=a(2 x-a) \\ & y^2=2 a\left(x-\frac{a}{2}\right) \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider a conic $C: y^2=4 x$. Let $\triangle P Q R$ be an equilateral triangle with side length $k$ where $P$ be any point on $C, Q$ be the foot of perpendicular from $P$ upon the directrix of $C$ and $R$ be the focus of $C$. A circle $C_2$ is inscribed in another conic $C_1: y^2=k(x+1)$ which touches $C_1$ at the points where $C_1$ cuts the $y$-axis. $C_3$ is an ellipse whose auxiliary circle is $C_2$ and major axis coincides with the axis of symmetry of $C_1$ and whose length of minor axis is 4.Question: Identify the correct statement(s) for $\mathrm{C}_1$.
options: Minimum length of focal chord is 4.
Locus of point of intersection of perpendicular tangents is $x+2=0$.
Distance between focus and tangent at vertex is 1.
questions.solution:
We have $P M=1+t^2$ $\begin{aligned} & P S=\sqrt{\left(t^2-1\right)^2+4 t^2}=\left(t^2+1\right) \\ & M S=\sqrt{4+4 t^2}=2 \sqrt{1+t^2} \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{1+t^2}=1+t^2 \end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad P M=1+t^2=4=a=k \text { (Given) }$ Hence $C_1: y^2=4(x+1)$ Equation of tangent to $C_1$ at $(0,2)$ is $\begin{aligned} & 2 y=4\left(\frac{x+0}{2}+1\right) \\ & \Rightarrow y=x+2 \end{aligned}$ Now circle which touches above line at $(0,2)$, is $x^2+(y-2)^2+\lambda(x-y+2)=0$. As above circle is passing through the point $(0,-2)$, so
$\begin{aligned} & 0+16+\lambda(4)=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=-4 \\ & \therefore \quad C_2: x^2+(y-2)^2-4(x-y+2)=0 \\ & \text { or, } C_2: x^2+y^2-4 x-4=0\end{aligned}$ Now $C_3: \frac{(x-2)^2}{\mathrm{a}^2}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{\mathrm{~b}^2}=1, \quad \mathrm{a}=2 \sqrt{2}$ and $\mathrm{b}=2$ So $\mathrm{C}_3: \frac{(\mathrm{x}-2)^2}{8}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{4}=1$. (i) Given $\mathrm{C}_1: \mathrm{y}^2=4(\mathrm{x}+1)$ (A) Minimum length of focal chord $=$ Latus rectum $=4$. (B) Locus of point of intersection of perpendicular tangents $=$ Director circle which is $\mathrm{x}+2=0$. (C) Clearly distance between focus and tangent at vertex is 1. (D) Foot of the directrix is clearly $(-2,0)$. (ii) We have $C_3: \frac{(x-2)^2}{8}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$ (A) $e=\sqrt{1-\frac{4}{8}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ (B) Focal length $=2$ ae $=2 \times 2 \sqrt{2}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=4$ (C) Latus-rectum $=\frac{2 b^2}{a}=2\left(\frac{4}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)=2 \sqrt{2}$ (D) Director circle is $(x-2)^2+y^2=12 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-8=0$
(iii) Triangle formed by common tangents to the curves $\mathrm{C}_1$ and $\mathrm{C}_2$ and latus-rectum of $\mathrm{C}_1$, is isosceles triangle. Required area $=\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 2=4$ square units.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider a conic $C: y^2=4 x$. Let $\triangle P Q R$ be an equilateral triangle with side length $k$ where $P$ be any point on $C, Q$ be the foot of perpendicular from $P$ upon the directrix of $C$ and $R$ be the focus of $C$. A circle $C_2$ is inscribed in another conic $C_1: y^2=k(x+1)$ which touches $C_1$ at the points where $C_1$ cuts the $y$-axis. $C_3$ is an ellipse whose auxiliary circle is $C_2$ and major axis coincides with the axis of symmetry of $C_1$ and whose length of minor axis is 4.Question: The triangle formed by common tangents to curves $C_1$ and $C_2$ and latus rectum of $C_1$, is
questions.solution:
We have $P M=1+t^2$ $\begin{aligned} & P S=\sqrt{\left(t^2-1\right)^2+4 t^2}=\left(t^2+1\right) \\ & M S=\sqrt{4+4 t^2}=2 \sqrt{1+t^2} \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{1+t^2}=1+t^2 \end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad P M=1+t^2=4=a=k \text { (Given) }$ Hence $C_1: y^2=4(x+1)$ Equation of tangent to $C_1$ at $(0,2)$ is $\begin{aligned} & 2 y=4\left(\frac{x+0}{2}+1\right) \\ & \Rightarrow y=x+2 \end{aligned}$ Now circle which touches above line at $(0,2)$, is $x^2+(y-2)^2+\lambda(x-y+2)=0$. As above circle is passing through the point $(0,-2)$, so
$\begin{aligned} & 0+16+\lambda(4)=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=-4 \\ & \therefore \quad C_2: x^2+(y-2)^2-4(x-y+2)=0 \\ & \text { or, } C_2: x^2+y^2-4 x-4=0\end{aligned}$ Now $C_3: \frac{(x-2)^2}{\mathrm{a}^2}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{\mathrm{~b}^2}=1, \quad \mathrm{a}=2 \sqrt{2}$ and $\mathrm{b}=2$ So $\mathrm{C}_3: \frac{(\mathrm{x}-2)^2}{8}+\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{4}=1$. (i) Given $\mathrm{C}_1: \mathrm{y}^2=4(\mathrm{x}+1)$ (A) Minimum length of focal chord $=$ Latus rectum $=4$. (B) Locus of point of intersection of perpendicular tangents $=$ Director circle which is $\mathrm{x}+2=0$. (C) Clearly distance between focus and tangent at vertex is 1. (D) Foot of the directrix is clearly $(-2,0)$. (ii) We have $C_3: \frac{(x-2)^2}{8}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$ (A) $e=\sqrt{1-\frac{4}{8}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ (B) Focal length $=2$ ae $=2 \times 2 \sqrt{2}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=4$ (C) Latus-rectum $=\frac{2 b^2}{a}=2\left(\frac{4}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)=2 \sqrt{2}$ (D) Director circle is $(x-2)^2+y^2=12 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-8=0$
(iii) Triangle formed by common tangents to the curves $\mathrm{C}_1$ and $\mathrm{C}_2$ and latus-rectum of $\mathrm{C}_1$, is isosceles triangle. Required area $=\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 2=4$ square units.
